nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.name: Ornate hawk-eagle
nodes.summary: Ornate hawk-eagle（Ornate hawk-eagle，学名 Spizaetus ornatus），在图谱中标记为极危（CR）物种，主要分布于原始森林，主要威胁包括森林砍伐、人类活动扰动、小种群风险等4项。

nodes.name: 原始森林
nodes.summary: 原始森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联240种鸟类，包括黑額鳴冠雉、Singing quail、Stripe-faced wood quail等240种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: CR
nodes.summary: CR表示 IUCN 极危等级，当前关联174种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、加州神鹫、菲律宾鹰等174种鸟类。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |year=2001 |title=Raptors of the World |publisher=Houghton Mifflin Harcourt |isbn=0-618-12762-3}} This species is found largely in primary forests with tall trees, although can be found in many forest types. The ornate hawk-eagle female lays almost always a single egg and the species has a fairly prolonged breeding cycle like many tropical raptors, especially due to a lengthy post-fledging stage on which juveniles are dependent on their parents.Phillips, R

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The Condor, 90(1), 239-241. Like many forest-dependent raptors, especially those in the tropical and subtropical regions, this species is likely under the pressing threat of deforestation

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Therefore, in 2012, the species was uplisted to Near Threatened. Currently the total population is estimated at between 13,300 and 33,300 individuals, a very small population considering the species' ample range. Various means of analysis in the Atlantic Forest of Brazil all showed it to be least densely populated of the three species of Spizaetus in the area. In particular, when compared to the black hawk-eagle, the ornate hawk-eagle is much less tolerant of partial deforestation and human disturbance and is generally rather more scarce. For example, in the fragmented forest of Paranapiacaba, Brazil, the black hawk-eagle was recorded 2.5 times more frequently during surveys than the ornate species.Mañosa, S., Mateos, E., Pedrocchi, V., & Martins, F

links.label: 受威胁于

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Journal of Raptor Research, 47(4), 392-400. Beyond deforestation, human hunting is also causing declines of this species. In part this is due to competition for resources
